steps.text: Determine if the facility radiation protection program has been maintained in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee commitments, since the last inspection.
Determine if the licensee’s procedures for radiation protection at the facility meet regulatory requirements, and licensee commitments.
Determine if Form NRC-3, 'Notice to Employees,' is posted as required.
Determine if restricted areas are posted and controlled as required.
Determine if appropriate protective clothing is used by facility personnel as required.
Determine if the licensee’s personnel dosimetry program is effective, maintained and meets regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if licensee personnel, equipment and materials exiting a controlled area are monitored as required.
Determine if personnel working with or around radioactive materials have been instructed in radiation protection as required.
Determine if required radiation surveys, sampling and monitoring were performed in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee procedures.
Determine if the licensee has implemented a calibration program for the survey, sampling and monitoring instruments in use as required.
Determine if exposure limits in 10 CFR Part 20 were satisfied.
Determine if changes with the potential to affect radiation protection activities were consistent with 10 CFR 50.59, the license and Technical Specification (TS) requirements.
Determine if the licensee addressed radiologically significant events in accordance with regulatory and the licensee’s procedural requirements.
Determine if an ALARA program has been implemented as required.
Determine if the licensee has developed, documented and implemented a radiation protection program in accordance with regulatory requirements.
Determine if the respiratory protection program meets regulatory requirements and licensee commitments.
Determine if planned special exposures were made in accordance with regulatory requirements and licensee procedures.
Determine if the dose to the embryo/fetus for declared pregnant women met regulatory requirements.
Post NRC-3 conspicuously.
Ask the licensee where NRC-3 forms are posted and observe the postings.
Observe controls for two radiologically controlled areas.

constraints.text: Radiologically controlled areas are required to be conspicuously posted.
High radiation areas are required to be strictly controlled to prevent unauthorized or inadvertent access.
If volatile radioactive materials are used, it must be posted and controlled for airborne contamination.

preconditions.text: Do not enter a high radiation area without a licensee escort trained in radiation protection who is knowledgeable of the high radiation area and the health physics precautions to be taken.

constraints.text: Control of high radiation areas is required.

steps.text: Observe the protective clothing worn by two facility experimenters or other respective personnel during their work activities.
Discuss with two individuals to determine their use of protective clothing.
Ensure personnel are instructed in the proper use and function of the protective clothing in accordance with 10 CFR 19.12.
Determine if the licensee has provisions for the correct disposition of contaminated protective clothing.
Observe the personnel dosimetry worn by four facility workers, including one operator and one experimenter.
Ensure dosimetry devices issued to facility personnel are appropriate for the type and energy of the emitted radiation and the radiation fields anticipated.
Ensure facility personnel required to use dosimeters are instructed in the proper use and function of the dosimeters.

constraints.text: Requirements for protective clothing may be found in the licensee’s procedures or on precautions posted at the entrance to controlled areas.
Some articles of protective clothing may be held for radioactive decay depending upon the contaminants and their half-lives.

steps.text: Wear dosimetry as required.
Check alarming dosimeters before each operation.
Assess the need for extremity monitors for handling activated samples.
Observe two selected facility workers leaving the controlled area to check if dosimeter readings are recorded.
Review the process for processing film or TLD badges.

constraints.text: Ensure dosimetry meets the requirements of 10 CFR 20.1501 for accreditation.

steps.text: Conduct an exit survey of four facility workers leaving a controlled area.
Check all personnel, tools, equipment, and protective clothing for contamination before leaving a controlled area.
Perform exit monitoring using hand held friskers, stand-in monitors, portal monitors, or hand and foot monitors.
Ensure the exit monitor is shielded from extraneous radiation sources.
Use a sensitive Geiger-Muller (GM) counter to detect beta/gamma emitters.
Monitor hands before removing the probe of hand held friskers.
Detect alpha contamination using a hand held gas flow proportional counter or smear survey and analyses.
Take nasal smears if personnel contamination by alpha emitters is suspected.
Evaluate the radiation safety training received by two permanent facility workers, and where applicable, two experimenters or students.
Observe respective activities and discuss the radiation safety training received by these individuals.
Confirm that appropriate training was actually received and understood.
Ensure that experimenters and students understand the radiation protection requirements associated with their assigned activities.
Determine if the instruction provided was appropriate to the tasks to be performed and if there was adequate supervision of their activities.
Observe two radiological surveys, sampling or monitoring activities, other than exit surveys.
Accompany a person qualified in the licensee’s radiation control techniques on normal rounds.
Request to be shown the manner in which tasks are normally accomplished.
Determine whether personnel conducting these activities are qualified by training and experience.
Perform operability checks on all instrumentation before use, e.g., source and battery checks.
Take smears or instrument readings in areas that are readily accessible to facility personnel.
Examine the licensee’s training records to confirm that technicians are sufficiently trained.
Check if surveys were performed as required by the licensee’s procedures.
Verify that the surveys indicate exposure rates are consistent with the licensee’s ALARA program.

constraints.text: All instrumentation used during these activities are required to be operable, calibrated, and maintained.
Particular attention is to be given to beam ports, experimental stations, and ion exchangers for cleanup of primary water, where applicable.

preconditions.text: If radiation levels above background are suspected in areas outside of the restricted area, determine whether radiation levels are above those specified in 10 CFR Part 20, Subpart D.
If contamination levels exceed double the background, a barrier should be suggested unless the contamination is removable and the area is immediately decontaminated.

steps.text: Examine surveys of solid waste material to determine whether the licensee complies with their procedures and requirements.
Verify the calibration of radiation monitoring instrument(s) used in the licensee’s radiation surveys.
Examine the technical adequacy of calibration procedures at facilities that perform their own calibrations.

preconditions.text: If the potential exists for airborne contamination, ensure that levels are within the regulatory requirements of 10 CFR Part 20, Subparts C and D.
If individuals routinely exceed 20 percent of the exposure limits, the issue is to be reviewed further to determine that the radiation exposure is consistent with the licensee’s ALARA program.
If there were exposures greater than the 10 CFR Part 20 limits, determine if the licensee reported the over exposures in accordance with 10 CFR Parts 20.2202 and 20.2203.
Review changes in organization, personnel, facilities, equipment, programs, and procedures that could affect radiation protection activities since the last inspection.

constraints.text: 10 CFR 20.1702 requires the use of respiratory protection equipment (and other controls) to limit intakes of radioactive material consistent with ALARA.

Although planned special exposures are not generally expected at non-power reactors, 10 CFR 20.1201(b), 20.1206, 20.2104(b) and(e)(2), 20.2105, 20.2106, 20.2202(e), and 20.2204 provide requirements, as appropriate, for planned special exposures.
